content: Combining the structure of Self-Refine's iterative unsupervised refinement framework with multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms creates a two-layer self-improvement pipeline: after the LLM generates an initial output, it first leverages inconsistent contrasting perspectives from self-reflection to surface unrecognized flaws, then runs these targeted critiques through Self-Refine's iterative feedback loop to refine outputs without any additional training or labeled data. This hybrid approach resolves a key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine, which often gets stuck in local optimization loops because it only critiques outputs from its original generating perspective.
